description_long: This sculpture depicts two figures side by side, one with a human form and the other with a lioness or feline head, both standing upright on a rectangular base. The figures are rendered in a classical Egyptian style, with detailed headdresses and stylized hair. The left figure is seated and holds a tablet or inscribed object on its lap, while the right figure stands with arms at its sides. The surface is predominantly smooth with some areas showing signs of wear or damage, particularly on the lower portions. The sculpture is carved from a light-colored stone, likely limestone or marble, and features intricate details in the hair and facial features. The overall form is blocky and solid, with the figures closely joined and sharing a single base.
description: A dual-figure Egyptian-style sculpture featuring a standing lioness-headed figure and a seated human figure, both on a rectangular stone base. The piece is detailed, solid, and carved from light-colored stone.
description_view_0: Front view showing the lioness-headed figure standing upright, with the human figure partially visible behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the seated human figure holding an inscribed tablet, with the standing figure in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of both figures, with the headdresses and hair details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the seated and standing figures, with the inscribed tablet visible.
attribute_subject_matter: Egyptian deities or figures, one with a lioness head and one human
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
